title: sorbus aria f. longifolia
intro: Sorbus aria f. longifolia, also known as Whitebeam or Mountain Ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to the presence of salicylic acid. The health benefits of Sorbus aria f. longifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for various forms of consumption in traditional folk medicine and herbalism.
